fact: June 24 is exactly 3 days after the summer solstice in the Northern Hemisphere, which means you're still getting some of the longest days of the entire year!
In Antarctica right now, it's the middle of winter and the sun barely peeks above the horizon - some places get only 4 hours of dim twilight!
Fireflies are at their absolute peak right now in North America - they're flashing like crazy to find mates during these warm June nights!
Why do strawberries taste so amazing right now? Because June 24 falls right in the middle of peak strawberry season in most temperate regions!
Baby sea turtles are hatching on beaches around the world right now, using the bright moon to guide them to the ocean for the first time!
June 24 falls smack in the middle of Cancer season - people born today are Cancers, represented by the crab!
Mosquitoes are at their most annoying right now because the warm, humid weather creates perfect breeding conditions in puddles and ponds!
In northern countries like Alaska and Norway, the sun might not set until after 11 PM tonight - or might not set at all!
Rose bushes are exploding with their second flush of blooms right now, making gardens smell absolutely incredible!
June 24 is day 175 of the year, which means there are exactly 190 days left until New Year's Eve!
Thunderstorms are most likely to happen in the late afternoon today because the ground has been heating up all day, creating perfect conditions for big, booming storms!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest right now - the hot weather makes these bugs super active and extra noisy during the day!
